questions.title: Stage definitions
questions.text: What are your organization's opportunity stage names and their business semantics? For example: does 'Closed Won' mean a contract is signed, payment is received, or revenue is recognized? Does 'Negotiation' imply a verbal agreement or just a pricing discussion?

choices.text: Standard Salesforce stages with default meanings (Prospecting, Qualification, Proposal, Negotiation, Closed Won/Lost)
Custom stage names that diverge from standard Salesforce defaults
Standard stages but with organization-specific semantic definitions that differ from default Salesforce behavior
All of the above
Other (please specify)

questions.title: Weighted amount logic
questions.text: How should pipeline value be weighted or adjusted? For example: should amounts be multiplied by close probability, assigned fixed stage-based weights, use a hybrid model, or some other transformation?

choices.text: Probability-weighted: amount × probability percentage
Fixed stage weights: each stage has a predefined percentage (e.g., Negotiation = 80%, Proposal = 50%)
Hybrid: probability-weighted for early stages, fixed weights for late stages
Raw amount: unadjusted pipeline value
Other (please specify)

questions.title: Booking definition
questions.text: What defines a 'booking'? When does an opportunity count as a booking versus a pipeline deal? (e.g., Closed Won only, signed NDA, verbal commitment, finance recognition, or something else?)

choices.text: Closed Won in Salesforce equals a booking
Booking occurs when finance recognizes revenue (may differ from Closed Won timing)
Booking requires a signed contract or order form (Closed Won + documentation)
Booking is a verbal or written commitment before contract execution
Other (please specify)

questions.title: Forecast categories
questions.text: What forecast categories does your organization use, and how do they relate to opportunity stages? (e.g., Commit, Best Case, Pipeline, Omitted — and do they map directly to stages or are they separate fields?)

choices.text: Standard Salesforce forecast categories mapped directly to stages (Commit = Closed Won, Best Case = late-stage, Pipeline = early/mid-stage)
Custom forecast categories that override or supplement standard Salesforce defaults
Forecast categories are not used; pipeline is reported by stage only
All of the above
Other (please specify)

questions.title: Sales reporting hierarchy
questions.text: What is the grain of your sales reporting hierarchy? (e.g., rep-level, team-level, region-level, or something else?) Who rolls up to whom for pipeline and booking reporting?

choices.text: Rep-level only: each rep's opportunities roll directly to management
Team-level: reps grouped into teams with shared quota and reporting
Region or business unit-level: multi-level hierarchy (rep → team → region → division)
All of the above
Other (please specify)

notes.title: Salesforce as source
notes.body: The user context mentions Salesforce exports as current source data but explicitly states this is not an extraction or file-ingestion skill. Questions should focus on business semantics, not export mechanics or API behavior.

notes.title: Reconciliation scope
notes.body: Q3 (booking definition) is critical for reconciliation to finance bookings. The booking definition drives whether pipeline-to-bookings reconciliation is a simple stage filter or a more complex multi-system comparison.
